curation_notes: The advisory describes a SQL injection in the content_title parameter of /cms/content/list. The web ContentAction.list() assembles all request parameters into a Map via BasicUtil.assemblyRequestMap(), then passes it to ContentBizImpl.list(). ContentBizImpl.list() retrieves an 'arclist' SQL FreeMarker template from the database and calls ParserUtil.rendering(map, sqlFtl) to interpolate user-controlled values directly into raw SQL, which is executed via tagBiz.queryForList(sql) without parameterized binding. The actual fix is in the ms-base/ms-basic dependency upgrade from 3.0.1 to 3.0.2, which sanitizes input in ParserUtil.rendering(). No sibling files in the same biz/impl directory use the same FreeMarker-to-raw-SQL pattern. Pre-assigned class sql-injection is correct per CWE-89, the advisory description, and the code pattern.
